🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Craigievar Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 17th century
*Coords:* 57.1742, -2.7181
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Craigievar_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Craigievar%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNuvNlHIIugQ10ozjVVpMJ6sdVPQ5cFnCzkt0vhWG13-3GlkmVRaSMWoo798O5Zu22Z01BA5uvdh-Tqi8uV2i9qhmkYdxFOcOmHcdOrj5BcrUVHaFgjGwYQTl4ZZT-tqilO0ipAsBXuL2moyzRPgZrDEg=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Craigievar Castle is a harled castle or fortified country house 6 miles (9.7 km) south of Alford, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It was the seat of Clan Sempill, and the Forbes family resided here for 350 years until 1963, when the property was sold to the National Trust for Scotland by the 19th Lord Sempill.

_Listing 1 of 10_

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Castle Fraser
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.203, -2.46055556
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Castle_Fraser
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Castle%20Fraser%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZP0-jJiKu50G4ygJzsGOVBNl-B6OmMqCQ2GqjECMMeTFKXAxeM8_JZwiBHSKWnV1CnDlg5rfwMbTJYyOqOOhyKCj76xrvYSBP5U1q95yV349KP9rAjXPfhjRwXsCRV3ewQ7HEn7TNFSoWII_A=s4800-w1080
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Castle Fraser is a large Z-plan castle near Kemnay, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It is considered one of the most elaborate examples of its type and one of the grandest of the 'Castles of Mar'. The castle stands in over 300 acres (1.2 km2) of landscaped grounds, woodland and farmland, including a 19th-century walled kitchen garden. There is archaeological evidence of an older square tower dating from around 1400 or 1500 within the current construction. The castle is a Category A listed building and the grounds are included in the Inventory of Gardens and Designed Landscapes in Scotland.

_Listing 2 of 10_

listing.name: Castle Fraser
listing.desc: Castle Fraser is a large Z-plan castle near Kemnay, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It is considered one of the most elaborate examples of its type and one of the grandest of the 'Castles of Mar'. The castle stands in over 300 acres (1.2 km2) of landscaped grounds, woodland and farmland, including a 19th-century walled kitchen garden. There is archaeological evidence of an older square tower dating from around 1400 or 1500 within the current construction. The castle is a Category A listed building and the grounds are included in the Inventory of Gardens and Designed Landscapes in Scotland.

*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside

*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 57.061483, -2.439917
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crathes_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Crathes%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNLoxXr1R1BwI1T8d--cOkvo9T7LwdgBlRKuGMllWkT7PUGH_sR6c0uF34UT_FCSpNHIqsVMPvI7fMX0ZZRcQnWLhXluMWiGJivn9BDDd7XHo5aQQaZ3-zF436qQdS93wa_5Z55Heg1H8IE0A=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Crathes Castle is a castle built in the 16th century, near Banchory in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. It is in the historic county of Kincardineshire. This harled castle was built by the Burnetts of Leys and was owned by the family for almost 400 years. The castle and its grounds are currently owned and managed by the National Trust for Scotland and are open to the public.

_Listing 3 of 10_

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Drum Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 57.09503, -2.33794
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drum_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Drum%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/places/ANXAkqEbIsbaqoRe4TbnzvRrI0xGv66bv1x9-sJm5mU3NW-eQ7yJUPa4N6eT3HXnL7cWsYrPcpFa7RczwLI1vzPxweImHc8K4dADm7E=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Drum Castle is a castle near Drumoak in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. For centuries it was the seat of the chief of Clan Irvine. The place-name Drum is derived from Gaelic druim, 'ridge'. The site is located approximately 6+1⁄2 miles northeast of Banchory and 3 miles west of Peterculter. The property is now owned by the National Trust for Scotland and is open to the public.

_Listing 4 of 10_

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Fyvie Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* northeast tayside
*County:* Aberdeenshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 57.4433, -2.3949
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fyvie_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Fyvie%20Castle%20Aberdeenshire%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNAL7-VyjkwpnnDkwxXc6Uxl2NPcQ-e1iCtlnIctCiwLxGDdjViWq2EG4MVsn6h8Y93DAwIsncgAvbsBNvZJXqYZNdE7-dbzZrP9Bh1vqRbzB7WYLpQTAztj74aP4iIlzfqac6I-PSRHvNO_w=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Fyvie Castle is a castle in the village of Fyvie, near Turriff in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. The earliest parts of the castle date from the 13th century, and it later passed through the hands of several prominent families, including the Prestons, Setons and Gordons. Since 1984 it has been owned and managed by the National Trust for Scotland.

_Listing 5 of 10_

Delgatie Castle is a castle near Turriff, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. The castle consists of a white-harled, five-storey tower dating from the 16th century, with later extensions.
